description: Fix new ToolError('Request was aborted', 'aborted') with no category in jira-api.ts — abort errors are not categorized as timeout.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/jira && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Replace new ToolError('aborted') with ToolError.timeout() in jira-api.ts
userStories.description: In plugins/jira/src/jira-api.ts, the catch block at line 83 throws `new ToolError('Request was aborted', 'aborted')` with no category. Replace with ToolError.timeout() so abort errors are properly categorized.
acceptanceCriteria: The `new ToolError('Request was aborted', 'aborted')` line is replaced with ToolError.timeout('Request was aborted') in jira-api.ts
No direct ToolError constructor calls remain for abort errors
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/jira/src/jira-api.ts (line 83)

Context:
Line 83:
  if (err instanceof DOMException && err.name === 'AbortError') throw new ToolError('Request was aborted', 'aborted');

Change to:
  if (err instanceof DOMException && err.name === 'AbortError') throw ToolError.timeout('Request was aborted');

The 'network_error' line below uses { category: 'internal' } which is correctly set — leave that unchanged.
